content.text: Please do 7 out of the 11 problems below.

body.text: An algebraic extension field \(F\) of \(K\) is said to be normal over \(K\) if every irreducible polynomial in \(K[x]\) that has a root in \(F\) actually splits in \(F[x]\). Prove that an algebraic extension \(F\) of \(K\) is normal over \(K\) if and only if for every irreducible \(f\in K[x]\), \(f\) factors in \(F[x]\) as a product of irreducible factors all of which have the same degree.

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Show that the additive group of rationals \(\mathbb Q\) is not free.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Show that the group \(\mathbb Q^+\) of all positive rationals (under multiplication) is free abelian with basis \(\{p:p\text{ is prime in }\mathbb Z\}\).

body.text: Show that any simple group \(G\) of order 60 is isomorphic to \(A_5\). (If you want, you may use the fact that for each \(n\geq 2\), \(A_n\) is the only subgroup of \(S_n\) of index 2.)

Let \(R\) be an integral domain and for each maximal ideal \(M\), consider the localization \(R_M\) as a subring of the quotient field of \(R\). Show that \(\bigcap R_M=R\), where the intersection is taken over all maximal ideals \(M\) of \(R\).

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Prove that any group of order 48 is solvable (without using Burnside’s \((p,q)\)-Theorem).

body.text: Let \(R\) be a ring. Show that the following conditions on an \(R\)-module \(P\) are equivalent.

subparts.label: (ii).
subparts.text:
Every short exact sequence
\[
0\to A\xrightarrow{f}B\xrightarrow{g}P\to 0
\]
is split exact.

subparts.label: (iii).
subparts.text: There is a free module \(F\) and an \(R\)-module \(K\) such that \(F\cong K\oplus P\).

body.text: Let \(C\) and \(D\) be categories, and let \(S\) and \(T\) be covariant functors from \(C\) to \(D\).

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text:
If \(B\) is a unitary left module over a ring \(R\) with identity, show that there is a “natural” isomorphism of modules
\[
\alpha_B:R\otimes_R B\cong B.
\]

body.text: Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with identity.

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Let \(\mathcal A\) be an ideal in \(R\), and assume that \(M\) is a finitely generated \(R\)-module such that \(\mathcal A.M=M\). Show that there is some \(a\in\mathcal A\) satisfying \((1+a)M=0\).

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Let \(M\) be a finitely-generated \(R\)-module. Show that \(J(R).M=M\) implies \(M=\{0\}\). (Here, \(J(R)\) denotes the Jacobson radical of \(R\).)

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Show that if \(R\) is a unique factorization domain, then \(R\) is integrally closed.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Find the integral closure of \(\mathbb C[x^5,x^7]\).

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Show that \(\mathbb C[x,y]/(xy-1)\) (quotient of polynomial ring in 2 variables) is not isomorphic to \(\mathbb C[t]\) (polynomial ring in 1 variable).

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text:
Show that the set
\[
\{(m,n):m,n\in\mathbb Z\}
\]
is not an algebraic variety in \(\mathbb C^2\).

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Find the Galois group of \(K:\mathbb Q\), where \(K\) is a splitting field over \(\mathbb Q\) for \(t^4+t^2-6\).

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Write down the subgroups of the Galois group.

subparts.label: (c).
subparts.text: Write down the corresponding fixed fields.
